Common_Conditions: Chronic Kidney Disease, Diabetes, Hypertension, Polycystic Kidney Disease
Description: Most commonly transplanted organ. One kidney can be donated by a living donor.

Common_Conditions: Cirrhosis, Hepatitis B/C, Liver Cancer, Fatty Liver Disease
Description: Second most transplanted organ. Living donors can donate a portion that regenerates.

Common_Conditions: Heart Failure, Cardiomyopathy, Coronary Artery Disease, Congenital Heart Defect
Description: Only from deceased donors. Requires precise matching and rapid transport.

Common_Conditions: COPD, Pulmonary Fibrosis, Cystic Fibrosis, Pulmonary Hypertension
Description: One of the most challenging transplants. Single or double lung possible.
Often combined with kidney transplant for diabetic patients.

Common_Conditions: Short Bowel Syndrome, Intestinal Failure, Crohn's Disease
Description: Rarest organ transplant. Complex surgery with highest rejection risk.
Most commonly transplanted tissue. Very high success rate.

Common_Conditions: Leukemia, Lymphoma, Sickle Cell Disease, Aplastic Anemia
Description: Living donation through peripheral blood or bone marrow harvest.
